title: Open Wide
description: Test your understanding of the video and practise useful dentist vocabulary, body words and action verbs.

questions.question: What is a “waiting room”?
options: The room where patients sit before their appointment
The room where dentists keep their tools
The room where patients brush their teeth
The room where people pay for food
questions.answer: The room where patients sit before their appointment
questions.explanation: The waiting room is where Sune sits before seeing the dentist.

questions.question: What is an “appointment”?
options: A planned time to see someone, like a doctor or dentist
questions.answer: A planned time to see someone, like a doctor or dentist
questions.explanation: Sune is waiting before his dental appointment.

questions.question: What does “recline” mean?
options: To tilt back so you are lying down
questions.answer: To tilt back so you are lying down
questions.explanation: The dental chair reclines, meaning it tilts back.

questions.question: What does “examine” mean?
options: To look at something very carefully
questions.answer: To look at something very carefully
questions.explanation: The dentist examines Sune's teeth by looking carefully inside his mouth.

questions.question: What is a “cavity”?
options: A small hole in a tooth caused by decay
A tool used to clean the floor
questions.answer: A small hole in a tooth caused by decay
questions.explanation: A cavity is a small hole in a tooth.

questions.question: What does “numb” mean?
options: To lose feeling in a part of your body
questions.answer: To lose feeling in a part of your body
questions.explanation: The dentist gives Sune an injection to numb the area.

questions.question: What is your “gum”?
options: The soft pink tissue around your teeth
The hard white part of your tooth
questions.answer: The soft pink tissue around your teeth
questions.explanation: The injection goes into Sune's gum, the soft tissue around the teeth.

questions.question: What does “rinse” mean?
options: To swill water around your mouth
questions.answer: To swill water around your mouth
questions.explanation: Sune rinses his mouth with water before spitting.

questions.question: What does “swollen” mean?
options: Larger than normal because of injury or irritation
questions.answer: Larger than normal because of injury or irritation
questions.explanation: Sune walks out with a swollen cheek, meaning it is larger than normal.

sections.title: Video comprehension
sections.instructions: Answer the questions about the story.

questions.question: Where is Sune at the beginning of the video?
questions.explanation: Sune is sitting in the waiting room before his dental appointment.

questions.question: What happens when the nurse calls Sune's name?
options: He runs out of the building
questions.explanation: Sune stands up slowly because he is not excited about seeing the dentist.

questions.question: What tools does the dentist use to examine Sune's teeth?
options: A small mirror and a probe
questions.answer: A small mirror and a probe
questions.explanation: The dentist uses a small mirror and a probe to check Sune's teeth.

questions.question: What does the dentist use to numb the area?
questions.explanation: The dentist gives Sune an injection to numb the area.

questions.question: What does Sune think about while his mouth is wide open?
questions.explanation: Sune stares at the ceiling and thinks about lunch.

sections.title: Complete the sentence
sections.instructions: Choose the correct word or expression.

questions.question: The dentist asked me to sit in the dental chair, and then it started to ______.
questions.explanation: To recline means to tilt back so you are lying down.

questions.question: The dentist used a small mirror to ______ my teeth.
questions.explanation: To examine means to look at something very carefully.

questions.question: After the dentist visit, Sune walked out with a ______ cheek.
questions.explanation: Swollen means larger than normal, often because of injury or irritation.
